TranslationGlossary.title: Glossary
TranslationGlossary.langHint: Terms are grouped by target language: switch the language above to edit, and only the active target language's terms apply during translation
TranslationGlossary.exampleHint: Once a term is added, its source word is automatically rendered as the specified translation — no need to put the glossary in your system prompt
TranslationGlossary.subtitle: Pin fixed translations for names / jargon, applied per target language
TranslationGlossary.presetEmptyHint: No glossary yet — click + to create
TranslationGlossary.duplicateHint: Duplicate source terms found — only one entry per source term will apply
TranslationGlossary.noTermsForLang: No terms for this target language yet
TranslationGlossary.tsvHint: TSV format: one "source ⇥ target" pair per line, tab-separated — paste straight from a spreadsheet. Optional 3rd column: target language code (e.g. zh, ja) to import terms for multiple languages from one file

common.fileProcessFailed: Failed to process the file — check its encoding or try another
common.privacyNotice: Your API key and data are stored locally in your browser to ensure privacy. If you have any questions or suggestions, please contact us via the community or feedback page.
common.dragAndDropText: Click or drag file here to upload
common.noTargetLanguage: Select at least one target language.
common.singleFileModeTooltip: Keep only one file; new uploads replace the current file
common.hideResultsTooltip: Hide result preview to prevent page lag from large content
common.directExportTooltip: Automatically download after processing, skipping result preview
common.useCacheTooltip: Reuse previous translation cache to speed up processing
common.contextAwareTranslationTooltip: Translate with context for better coherence and accuracy (AI models only)
common.multiLanguageModeTooltip: Translate to multiple languages simultaneously, saved as separate files
common.resetUploadTooltip: Clear current input and uploaded files
common.sameLanguageTarget: Source and target languages are the same; automatically adjusted target to
common.sameLanguageSource: Source and target languages are the same; automatically adjusted source to
common.enterApiKey: Enter API Key or switch to Free API.
common.enterApiUrl: Please configure the API URL in 'API Settings'.
common.deepLXUnavailable: DeepLX service is temporarily unreachable, automatically switched to GTX API (Free).
common.deeplUnavailable: DeepL service is temporarily unreachable, please check the related settings in API Settings.
common.llmUnavailable: LLM service is temporarily unreachable, please check the related settings in API Settings.
common.noFileUploaded: Please upload a file to process first.
common.sourceTextPlaceholder: Enter or paste text to process...
common.noSourceText: Please enter or paste text content.
common.formatTooltip: Format: remove blank lines and trim leading/trailing spaces
common.resultToSourceTooltip: Overwrite input area with result text
common.readOnlyModeTooltip: Text is too long: the editor is temporarily read-only and shows a partial preview only. Your full content is unaffected — processing and export still use the entire text.
common.clearInputTooltip: Clear input content and uploaded files
common.removeCharsAfterTranslationTooltip: Automatically remove specified characters after translation (separate multiple with spaces)
common.multiLangSettingsHint: Enter language codes, separated by comma or space.
common.retryCountTooltip: Number of retry attempts when translation fails
common.requestTimeoutSecTooltip: Maximum wait per request (seconds). LLM thinking takes longer; 180s or more recommended.
common.customExportFilenameTooltip: '{'name'}' = original filename, '{'lang'}' = language, '{'ext'}' = extension, '{'date'}' = date, '{'time'}' = time; in multi-language mode '{'lang'}' is added automatically to avoid name clashes
common.partialFailureDesc: Failed lines kept the original text as a placeholder, so the file stays usable. These failures are usually network blips or temporary upstream hiccups — free services and self-hosted servers are especially prone to this, and it's not a problem with your setup. Click Retry to continue (successful lines come from cache, no re-translation); if it still fails, try again later or switch to a different translation service. Or click View failed lines to handle them manually.
common.failedLanguagesDesc: Every batch for these languages errored out — usually local LLM glitches, API rate limits, or the model refusing a specific language. Copy the codes and re-run targeting only them (paste into Quick Entry via Language Codes); the others will hit cache and skip.
common.gtxFreeAPIUnavailable: GTX Free service is temporarily unreachable — could be a network restriction. Please try again later, or switch to a different translation service.
common.edgeFreeAPIUnavailable: Edge Free service is temporarily unreachable — could be a network restriction. Please try again later, or switch to a different translation service.
common.translategemmaUnavailable: TranslateGemma service is temporarily unreachable, please make sure your local server (LM Studio / llama.cpp / Ollama) is running and the URL is correct.
common.networkUnavailable: Translation service is temporarily unreachable — could be a network issue or the service is having a hiccup. Please try again later, or switch to a different translation service.
common.translationTimeout: Translation request timed out — could be a slow network or the service is taking too long to respond. Please try again later, or switch to a different translation service.
common.translationTimeoutLocal: Local model translation timed out. A local LLM can get stuck in a repeat loop — lower max_tokens (the output cap) in Advanced Settings, and make sure the selected source language matches the actual text.
common.errorHint400: Invalid request — often a wrong model name or language code, or an unsupported parameter
common.errorHint401: API key is invalid or expired
common.errorHint402: Insufficient account balance — top up with your provider
common.errorHint403: Access forbidden — the key may be invalid, lack permission, or your region may be blocked
common.errorHint404: Endpoint or model not found — check the URL and model name
common.errorHint413: Input too large — reduce the chunk size or split the text
common.errorHint422: A parameter isn't supported by this model — often thinking/reasoning options
common.errorHint429: Rate limit exceeded, please retry later
common.errorHint456: Translation quota exhausted for this billing period
common.errorHintRelay: Network error (possibly CORS) — enable "API Relay" in API Settings
common.errorHintRelay403: The API returned 403 Forbidden — enable "API Relay" in API Settings
common.errorHintGeminiKey: Cannot reach Gemini: check your network first (some networks cannot reach Google services directly). If the network is fine, Google has been rejecting unrestricted API keys since June 19, 2026 — regenerate your key in AI Studio (restricted by default) or add a Gemini API restriction to the old key in Google Cloud Console
common.rateLimitCooldown: Rate limited — pausing briefly, will retry automatically

SubtitleTranslator.clientDescription: Upload subtitles or paste content. Select target language for fast translation. Supports batch processing and retains timelines.
SubtitleTranslator.bilingualTooltip: Generate both translated-only and bilingual versions
SubtitleTranslator.bilingualFormatTooltip: ASS: separate font/color styling for original and translation. SRT: plain text, both lines stacked in the same cue.
SubtitleTranslator.unsupportedSub: Unrecognized format. Please ensure file is SRT, ASS, VTT, SBV, or LRC.
SubtitleTranslator.noFileUploaded: Please upload a subtitle file first.
SubtitleTranslator.noSourceText: Please upload subtitle file or paste text.
SubtitleTranslator.noExtractedText: No subtitle content extracted, please check file format.
SubtitleTranslator.bilingualError: Complex style tags detected. Bilingual mode may cause formatting issues. Recommended to disable 'Bilingual Subtitles'.
SubtitleTranslator.assStyleTooltip: Customize font, size, color, and position of the bilingual ASS subtitle
SubtitleTranslator.assNativeModeTooltip: Native ASS bilingual: keep the source file's styles, or discard them and restyle with ASS Style (dialogue subtitles only)
SubtitleTranslator.assFontPlaceholder: Leave empty = auto by target language
SubtitleTranslator.assReadSystemFontsUnsupported: This browser can't read system fonts — type a font name instead

TranslationSettings.resetCacheConfirm: Clear all translation cache? This cannot be undone.
TranslationSettings.deeplxUrlExtra: Optional, leave empty to use our public server
TranslationSettings.urlOptionalExtra: Optional — leave empty to use the default endpoint
TranslationSettings.customApiHelp: Works with any OpenAI-compatible API endpoint — local servers (Ollama / LM Studio / vLLM) or cloud aggregators (Together AI / Fireworks AI / etc).
TranslationSettings.modelExtra: LLM Model Name, check API docs for available models
TranslationSettings.temperatureExtra: Temperature (0-2): Higher is more random, lower is more deterministic. Recommended: 0.2-0.8
TranslationSettings.systemPromptExtra: System prompt for LLM translation, leave empty for default
TranslationSettings.userPromptExtra: Custom translation prompt, leave empty for default. Variables:
TranslationSettings.chunkSizeExtra: Multi-line text (Subtitles, Markdown) will be split by this size
TranslationSettings.batchSizeExtra: Concurrent requests in non-context mode. Too high triggers API rate limits
TranslationSettings.contextBatchSizeExtra: Concurrent batches in context mode. Tune 'context lines' first — safer for speed. Raising this risks rate limits
TranslationSettings.maxTokensExtra: Cap on model output tokens. 0 = no cap (vendor default). Set 2048-4096 for local models (Ollama/llama.cpp) to fail-fast on repeat-loop hallucinations. Hitting the cap raises a loud error (no silent half-translation cached); if a legitimate long translation gets cut, raise this value.
TranslationSettings.contextWindowExtra: Lines per batch. Higher means fewer requests and more speed, but may exceed the model's token limit
TranslationSettings.useRelayTooltip: Enable API relay if you encounter CORS or 403 errors
TranslationSettings.useRelayOverridden: A custom URL is set — requests go directly to it and this switch has no effect; clear the URL to re-enable
TranslationSettings.useProxyTooltip: Route Custom LLM requests through /api/proxy to bypass browser CORS restrictions
TranslationSettings.useProxyOverridden: When enabled, the custom URL is sent to the server-side proxy as x-proxy-pass
TranslationSettings.reasoningEffortExtra: Off disables thinking (default); enabling is usually more accurate but slower and uses more tokens. Custom models also offer Auto = follow the model's built-in default (use only if Off triggers an API error).
TranslationSettings.presetEmptyHint: No presets yet — edit and click Save
TranslationSettings.presetDefaultReadonly: The default prompt is built-in — it can't be modified or deleted. Use "Save as new preset" to customize.
TranslationSettings.sendSystemPromptExtra: Turn off only when the chat template rejects the system role — typical error "Conversations must start with a user prompt" (e.g. Gemma family).
TranslationSettings.globalPromptsExtra: All LLM services share these prompts; switching services will not reset them

subtitleTranslator.title: Subtitle Translator
subtitleTranslator.description: Batch translate SRT, ASS, VTT, SBV, LRC subtitles. 120+ languages.
subtitleTranslator.metaTitle: Free Subtitle Translator | Batch SRT/VTT/ASS + Bilingual
subtitleTranslator.metaDescription: AI subtitle translator for SRT, ASS, VTT, SBV, LRC. 25+ engines: DeepSeek, Claude, GPT, DeepL. 120+ languages, preserved timecodes, bilingual.

faq.q: Which subtitle formats are supported? Can it translate SRT, ASS, VTT, SBV, and LRC?
faq.a: Five formats supported: SRT, ASS, VTT, SBV, and LRC. SRT/VTT fit YouTube, HTML5 players, and creator dashboards (export YouTube captions with yt-dlp); ASS/SSA fits Aegisub and anime fansubs, with line-leading position tags (e.g. \\an8) auto-restored after translation; SBV is the native export format of YouTube Studio's caption editor; LRC fits music lyrics. File encoding (UTF-8 / UTF-16 / GBK / Shift-JIS) is auto-detected via jschardet. Complex inline ASS effect tags (colors, animation, karaoke) are simplified during translation.

faq.q: Should I use machine translation or AI/LLM translation for subtitles? Which is best quality, which is cheapest?
faq.a: For cheap, use machine translation; for quality, use an AI/LLM. Machine translation (MT) is cheap or free but reads flat for dialogue: Google free tier costs nothing (long inputs get truncated); DeepL is top tier for European languages; Qwen-MT and Azure Translate are similar. AI/LLM bills per token but reads noticeably more natural — DeepSeek is the value pick for whole-season batches; Claude Sonnet or GPT-5 give the most natural dialogue; Gemini 3 Pro's 1M-token context handles book-length subtitles. Engine configs are shared with [JSON Translator](../json-translate) and [Markdown Translator](../md-translator).

faq.q: How do I create bilingual subtitles for YouTube or language learning?
faq.a: Select 'Bilingual' mode with your target language. Position translation above (for learning — focus on translation) or below (for consumption — primary is original). Export as ASS for colored dual-language subtitles, embed in Premiere/DaVinci Resolve, or upload to YouTube community subs, Vimeo, or any player supporting ASS format.

faq.q: How many subtitle files can I batch translate at once? What about a whole season of episodes?
faq.a: Batch upload an entire season's subtitles for simultaneous translation with no hard file limit (actual limit depends on your translation engine's concurrency and API quota). Each file translates and caches independently (IndexedDB), so refreshing the page doesn't lose translated files. For shared terminology across episodes (character names, place names), put a glossary in the System Prompt — all files share the same context, ensuring consistent translations across the entire season.

faq.q: How do I preserve names, brands, and proper nouns in subtitles so they aren't mistranslated?
faq.a: Add a glossary in the System Prompt; AI/LLM engines will respect it. Example: "Keep verbatim: iPhone, OpenAI, Premiere, person name John Smith." Especially recommended for TV series — all episodes share the same term dictionary, so the protagonist doesn't get called one name in episode 1 and a different one in episode 8. Machine translation (Google, DeepL, Qwen-MT) doesn't accept prompt customization, requires manual post-processing.

faq.q: How do I convert SRT to VTT or ASS? Do I need a separate subtitle format converter?
faq.a: No separate tool needed. Pick your target language and export format during translation for one-click conversion: SRT ↔ VTT, SRT/VTT → ASS, plus bilingual ASS/SRT output. VTT-to-SRT auto-handles inline <c.classname> tags and <00:00:06.040> karaoke timestamps; ASS conversion preserves \an8 position tags with default styling. Note: identical source and target language are blocked by validation, so format conversion currently requires an actual translation pass.

faq.q: Can I convert subtitles to plain-text scripts? Or export only specific lines?
faq.a: The 'Extract Text' button extracts the dialogue text without timecodes or cue numbers (auto-copied to clipboard) — useful for language learning materials, podcast scripts, AI training data, or voice-actor handoffs. For more granular line-level editing (filter by keyword, batch-replace terms, add prefix/suffix, sort/dedupe), pair with [Text Toolbox](../text-toolbox) — its regex matching and line operations cover most subtitle cleanup tasks. For splitting very long subtitles by time or character count, use [Text Splitter](../text-splitter).

faq.q: How do I fix subtitle timing mismatch or encoding (□□□) issues after translation?
faq.a: The tool only translates text and never modifies timecodes. Timing mismatches after translation usually stem from framerate inconsistencies (23.976 vs 25 fps) in the source — fix with Aegisub or Subtitle Edit. For encoding, jschardet auto-detects UTF-8/UTF-16/GBK/Shift-JIS; if detection fails it defaults to UTF-8, so re-save source as UTF-8 via Notepad++ or VS Code before uploading.

faq.q: Are subtitle files uploaded to your server? Is it private and secure?
faq.a: Fully client-side: subtitle file reading, parsing, and translation requests all happen in your browser — original content never touches our servers. LLM engine requests go directly from your browser to your configured API endpoint (OpenAI, DeepSeek, etc.), and API keys are stored only in local browser storage. Translation cache uses browser IndexedDB and persists across sessions. Source code is open on GitHub (rockbenben/subtitle-translator) for self-hosting or auditing.

features: Supports SRT, ASS, VTT, SBV, LRC subtitle formats, preserving ASS position tags (\\an8 etc.) after translation
Timecodes untouched — tool only translates text, original timing preserved
Bilingual subtitle position: above (learning) or below (viewing)
25+ translation engines: Google free, DeepSeek, Claude, GPT, Gemini, DeepL, Qwen-MT
Compatible with YouTube, Twitch, Vimeo, Premiere, DaVinci Resolve, Aegisub
